title: fumaria officinalis subsp. media
intro: Fumaria officinalis subsp. media, also known as the lesser fumitory, is a subspecies of the annual or biennial flowering plant Fumaria officinalis. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to its high content of alkaloids such as fumaritine and protopine. The lesser fumitory has been associated with various health benefits, including its potential to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and antispasmodic. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, and can be found in some traditional European folk medicine and homeopathic remedies.
